## Identity

- Name: Christopher Kenreigh
- Title: Director of UX & Product Design
- Location: USA — Open to Remote, Hybrid, or Relocation
- Portfolio: https://uxapex.com
- Email: c.kenreigh@gmail.com
- Phone: 480-206-2145
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/kenreigh/
- Availability: Actively seeking Director of UX, Director of Product Design, Head of Design, and VP of Design roles

---

## Who is Christopher Kenreigh?

Christopher Kenreigh is a design executive with 11+ years of experience building and leading design organizations across e-commerce, enterprise software, and industrial applications. He is based in the United States and is actively available for Director-level leadership roles in digital experience and product design — remote, hybrid, or relocation.

He is known for translating ambiguous strategic challenges into executable design direction, developing high-performing design teams, and communicating design value in business terms to C-suite and executive stakeholders.

He currently serves as Director of UX & Product Design at Plexus Worldwide, where he leads a cross-disciplinary team of 7 designers across UX, content, research, and interaction — responsible for US and three international e-commerce markets.

---

## Professional Summary

Christopher Kenreigh builds and leads design organizations at the intersection of strategy, business goals, and human-centered product thinking. His work spans three core areas:

1. Design Leadership — He builds and directs cross-disciplinary design teams (UX, content, interaction, research). At Plexus Worldwide he leads a team of 7 designers across US and 3 international e-commerce markets, with direct ownership of hiring, mentorship, project allocation, and design quality.

2. Design Operations & Systems — He architects the infrastructure that makes great design scalable: design systems, token-based component libraries, tooling ecosystems, and AI-augmented workflows. He consolidated 6 fragmented tools into one unified platform, cutting team inefficiency by 60%.

3. UX Strategy & Product Outcomes — He translates business strategy into shipped products with measurable outcomes: a 43% reduction in e-commerce checkout abandonment, a 35% increase in sprint delivery velocity via LLM-driven tooling, and a 75% improvement in POS transaction completion rates.

---

## Measurable Outcomes

- 43% reduction in checkout abandonment — Plexus Worldwide, mobile-first e-commerce UX redesign
- 60% increase in team efficiency — consolidated 6 fragmented design tools into 1 unified platform
- 35% increase in sprint delivery velocity — AI-augmented LLM design workflows
- 75% increase in POS transaction completion rates — OpenTech Alliance kiosk UX redesign
- 30% improvement in worker operational efficiency — Freeport-McMoRan industrial digital tools
- 50% reduction in internal data dissemination needs — Siemens, unified design systems across 4 industrial projects
- 65% improvement in product ease of use — Glynlyon, K-12 e-learning platform
- 3 international e-commerce markets launched — Plexus Worldwide, US plus 3 global regions

---

## Work History

1. UX Design Director — Plexus Worldwide (May 2024–Present) · USA
   - Directing 7-person cross-disciplinary design org across UX, content, research, and interaction
   - Owns team structure, project allocation, design quality, and direct 1:1 mentorship
   - Reduced checkout abandonment 43% through data-driven redesign informed by Power BI analytics
   - Consolidated 6 tools into unified platform cutting inefficiency 60%
   - Led AI-augmented workflow adoption increasing velocity 35%
   - Launched e-commerce across US and 3 international regions

2. UX Consultant — Independent Practice (Oct 2023–Jun 2024) · Remote
   - UX strategy and design systems consulting for e-commerce clients
   - Deepened expertise in AI-augmented prototyping and LLM-assisted research synthesis

3. Senior Manager Product Design — Freeport-McMoRan (Aug 2022–Oct 2023) · USA
   - Led end-to-end design for mining operation digital tools across web, iOS, and Android
   - Improved worker efficiency 30%
   - Facilitated 10 stakeholder workshops translating user insights into product requirements
   - Presented roadmaps to C-suite securing approval for 5 major releases
   - Implemented token-based UI design system across 3 platforms

4. Senior Manager Product Design — OpenTech Alliance (May 2021–Aug 2022) · USA
   - Secured budget for 3 new UX initiatives through C-suite presentations
   - Redesigned Point of Sale kiosk UI boosting transaction completion 75%
   - Improved e-commerce fulfillment experience 25%

5. UX Design Lead — Siemens (Jul 2020–May 2021) · USA
   - Unified design systems across 4 concurrent industrial projects
   - Reduced data dissemination needs 50%
   - Designed 3D and AutoCAD-integrated UI layouts for industrial control applications

6. Senior UX Designer — Glynlyon Inc (Feb 2014–Apr 2019) · USA
   - Increased K-12 e-learning platform ease of use 65%
   - Established WCAG and ADA compliance across all platforms
   - Led cross-functional Design Thinking workshops

---

## Core Capabilities

### Leadership & Team Development
Design org direction and team building, hiring and recruiting, mentorship and 1:1 coaching, design critique systems, cross-functional alignment, C-suite and executive communication, design maturity roadmaps, designer growth frameworks

### Strategy & Business Outcomes
UX strategy and roadmapping, OKR and business planning, design operations frameworks, workshop facilitation, stakeholder alignment, design maturity assessment, connecting design decisions to retention, conversion, and revenue

### UX & Product Design
End-to-end product design, design systems architecture, design tokens, mobile-first UX, interaction design, coded prototypes, e-commerce optimization, multi-region product launches, information architecture, journey mapping

### Research & Validation
User research, usability testing, A/B testing, Power BI and behavioral analytics, competitive analysis, design critique facilitation

### AI & Modern Tooling
AI-augmented design workflows, LLM-assisted research synthesis and prototyping, Figma, MUI/React, Google Material Design 3, Agile/Scrum methodology

---

## Design Philosophy

Christopher Kenreigh approaches design as a fundamentally business-oriented function. Every design decision must be defensible in terms of business outcomes — retention, conversion, and revenue — not pixels or deliverables.

He believes in building systems before screens: design systems, critique culture, and research processes that compound in value over time and enable teams to move fast without fragmenting quality.

His leadership philosophy centers on developing designers who graduate from needing approval to making autonomous, high-quality calls. He converts strategic ambiguity — incomplete requirements, misaligned stakeholders, undefined problems — into concrete design direction that teams can execute.

---

4. Rise of AI Suggestions: How Discovery is Evolving — Industry Wide — https://teststudynew.vercel.app/
5. Scaling Revenue Generation from Behavioral Metrics — E-commerce & Retail — https://persongratadata.vercel.app/

## Availability & Target Roles

Christopher Kenreigh is actively available for the following roles:
- Director of UX
- Director of Product Design
- Head of Design
- VP of Design

He is USA-based and open to remote, hybrid, or relocation. Industries of particular depth include e-commerce, enterprise software, and industrial applications, though his systems-thinking and leadership approach applies across sectors.
